avalancheActivity.comment:
Along the border with France light precipitation. As a consequence of warming moist and wet avalanches are possible as the day progresses, even large ones.

More recent wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant in all aspects and generally at elevated altitudes. This applies in particular on steep slopes, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. The avalanches can be released in the new snow and wind slab layers and reach large size.

Careful route selection and spacing between individuals are recommended.
avalancheActivity.highlights: The wind slabs remain for the foreseeable future prone to triggering at intermediate and high altitudes. Gradual increase in danger of moist and wet avalanches as the penetration by moisture increases.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In particular along the border with France in some localities up to 15 cm of snow will fall above approximately 2200 m. As a consequence of high temperatures and rain up to intermediate altitudes the snowpack can not consolidate. These weather conditions will give rise to extreme moistening of the snowpack over a wide area especially at low and intermediate altitudes.
Above approximately 1500 m a lot of snow is lying.
At intermediate and high altitudes the wind slabs will increase in size moderately.

avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and a moderate to strong wind from northwesterly directions, wind slabs will form over a wide area. This applies in particular in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain especially above the tree line.

New snow and wind slabs can be released, even by a single winter sport participant and reach large size.
The avalanche prone locations are barely recognisable because of the poor visibility. Meticulous route selection is required. Maintaining distances between individuals and one-at-a-time descents are recommended.

Additionally avalanches can also be released in deep layers and reach very large size in isolated cases in particular on shady slopes. This applies in particular in case of a large load.
avalancheActivity.highlights: Gradual increase in avalanche danger as a consequence of new snow and strong wind. This applies in particular in the regions exposed to heavier precipitation.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area over a wide area 20 to 40 cm of snow will fall above approximately 1500 m. As a consequence of new snow and a moderate to strong wind from northwesterly directions, further wind slabs formed in particular above the tree line. The new snow and wind slabs are lying on soft layers on wind-protected shady slopes.

Intermediate and high altitudes: Precarious weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack in particular on shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: Further increase in avalanche danger as a consequence of new snow and stormy weather.

avalancheActivity.comment:
The fresh snow as well as the wind slabs that are forming during the snowfall must be evaluated with care and prudence in all aspects above the tree line. The avalanche prone locations are to be found especially adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls and on steep slopes.
At elevated altitudes these avalanche prone locations are more prevalent. They are barely recognisable because of the poor visibility.

New snow and wind slabs can be released, even by a single winter sport participant or triggered naturally. Additionally avalanches can also be triggered in the old snowpack and reach large size.
In particular at low and intermediate altitudes medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large moist and wet avalanches are possible as the snowfall level rises.

Careful route selection and spacing between individuals are recommended.
avalancheActivity.highlights: As a consequence of new snow and wind the avalanche prone locations will become more prevalent as the day progresses. Above the tree line a considerable avalanche danger will prevail.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area over a wide area 15 to 25 cm of snow will fall above approximately 1800 m. The moderate wind will transport the new snow significantly. The wind slabs are lying on soft layers in particular on shady slopes.
Up to intermediate altitudes rain will fall over a wide area. These weather conditions will give rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack.

Individual weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack on shady slopes. This applies in particular at elevated altitudes.

tendency.highlights: As a consequence of new snow and wind there will be an increase in the danger.
